# apmask_weighted_cutout { #astroapers.apmask_weighted_cutout }

```python
apmask_weighted_cutout(weights, bbox, image, mask=None, fill_value=np.nan)
```

Return a bbox-tight weighted cutout for raw aperture weights and ``bbox``.

## Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

<code>[**weights**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [array_like]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Bbox-tight aperture weights with shape ``bbox.shape``.

<code>[**bbox**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [BoundingBox]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Location of the bbox-tight weights in ``image`` coordinates.

<code>[**image**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [array_like]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Two-dimensional image to sample.

<code>[**mask**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [array_like of bool]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [None]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   Boolean image mask with the same shape as ``image``. ``True`` pixels are excluded by setting their aperture weights to zero before multiplication.

<code>[**fill_value**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [float]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [np.nan]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   Value used in returned cutout pixels that do not overlap ``image``.

## Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

<code>[]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [ndarray]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Floating cutout with shape ``bbox.shape``. Overlapping pixels contain ``image * weight``; non-overlapping pixels contain ``fill_value``.
